title: Add ETag / If-None-Match revalidation to /api/tracks
description: Reduce wire bytes on UI tab switches: send ETag header derived from master.db mtime; client revalidates via If-None-Match; server returns 304 on match.
acceptanceCriteria: Subsequent GET with If-None-Match: "<master.db_mtime>" returns 304 Not Modified with empty body and no DB or snapshot read.
ETag changes when master.db mtime changes; client request with old ETag returns 200 + new payload.
Frontend stores last seen ETag and sends it on subsequent /api/tracks requests (UI part below).

steps.details: /api/tracks handler: response.headers['ETag'] = f'"{int(current_mtime)}"' (integer seconds is enough granularity). Check request.headers.get('if-none-match') against the same value; if match -> return Response(status_code=304).

docs/index.html: AppState._tracksEtag stored in module scope (not localStorage - per-session). On fetch /api/tracks, send headers If-None-Match: <stored_etag>. On 304, reuse the cached parsedTracks. On 200, store the new ETag from response.headers.get('ETag').
tests/test_serve_routes.py: first call returns 200 + ETag; second call with If-None-Match returns 304 + empty body. Vitest test for tests/web/ui-logic.test.js: fetch + ETag round-trip.

technicalNotes: FastAPI normalizes header lookups case-insensitively but emits them as-is - use 'ETag' canonical casing.
GZipMiddleware (existing) compresses the 200 path; 304 has no body so compression is moot.
